vulnerablePopulations: children and adults with frequent URIs
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis; bacterial sinusitis suggested by persistent symptoms >10 days, severe onset with fever/purulence, or double-worsening; imaging reserved for complications

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with symptomatic care and antibiotic stewardship

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS diagnosis; limited imaging; stewardship important to reduce unnecessary antibiotics
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Symptomatic care (analgesics, saline irrigation); antibiotics for likely bacterial cases; manage allergic rhinitis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antibiotic overuse and limited ENT access for recurrent cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Symptomatic management feasible in primary care and clear clinical criteria for antibiotics
